description: This figure presents scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images showing typical morphologies of aluminum nitride (AlN) inclusions in high-nitrogen (HN) Fe–28Mn–10Al–0.8C low-density steel. Panel (a) displays a single, intact AlN inclusion exhibiting a characteristic polyhedral structure with straight edges and sharp angles, measuring approximately 2–3 μm according to the 2 μm scale bar. Panel (b) reveals a fragmented AlN inclusion, demonstrating that these brittle ceramic particles can be broken during the multidirectional hot forging process (conducted at 1123–1323 K). The caption and citation context indicate that while fine AlN inclusions (84% smaller than 2 μm) promote austenite grain refinement through heterogeneous nucleation, the fractured AlN inclusions visible in panel (b) tend to serve as crack initiation sites during deformation. This microstructural evidence supports the paper's conclusion that nitrogen addition increases AlN inclusion number density (133 mm⁻² in HN vs. 73 mm⁻² in LN steel) and explains the trade-off between enhanced grain refinement and potential crack nucleation in high-nitrogen low-density steels.
